questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must Kentucky licensees complete per biennial renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Kentucky requires 12 hours of CE per biennial (2-year) renewal cycle. At least 6 hours must be completed per year, and at least 3 per year must cover real estate law topics.

questions.question_text: A licensee completes all 12 CE hours in year one of the renewal cycle and none in year two. Is this acceptable?
questions.correct_answer: No, minimum 6 hours per year required
distractors: Yes, the total is what matters
Only if 6 hours are in law topics
questions.explanation: Kentucky requires a minimum of 6 CE hours per year. Licensees cannot defer all 12 hours to a single year of the biennial cycle.

questions.question_text: How many CE hours per year must be in real estate law topics?
questions.explanation: Kentucky requires at least 3 hours of CE per year in real estate law topics. This ensures licensees stay current on legal developments affecting practice.

questions.question_text: A licensee completed a CE course in January. Can they take the same course again in November of the same year?
questions.correct_answer: No, same course cannot repeat in one year
distractors: Yes, if it covers required law topics
Yes, if taken more than 6 months apart
Only if the course content was updated
questions.explanation: Kentucky prohibits completing the same CE course twice in the same year. Licensees must take different courses to fulfill their annual requirements.

questions.question_text: What is the penalty for a Kentucky licensee who fails to complete required CE?
distractors: License placed in inactive status only
questions.explanation: Kentucky imposes a $1,000 fine and license cancellation for failure to complete CE requirements. This is one of the harsher penalties among states.

questions.question_text: Kentucky real estate licenses must be renewed by what date?
questions.explanation: Kentucky real estate license renewal is due by March 31 on a biennial (every 2 years) cycle. Failure to renew results in inactive status.

questions.question_text: What is the Kentucky real estate license renewal fee?
questions.explanation: The Kentucky real estate license renewal fee is $60 per biennial cycle, due by March 31.

questions.question_text: A licensee misses the March 31 renewal deadline. What is their license status?
questions.explanation: Failure to renew by March 31 results in inactive status. The licensee must complete CE requirements and pay any applicable penalties to reactivate.

questions.question_text: How does a Kentucky licensee process their license renewal?
distractors: By mailing a paper form to KREC
At a local PSI testing center
questions.explanation: KREC provides an eServices online portal for license renewals, applications, and other transactions. This streamlines the process electronically.

questions.question_text: The Kentucky Core Course is required how often, and what CE credit does it satisfy?
questions.correct_answer: Every 4 years; 3 hrs law + 3 hrs elective
distractors: Every 2 years; 6 hrs of law topics only
Every 4 years; 6 hrs of elective credit only
Annually; 3 hrs of required law topics
questions.explanation: The Kentucky Core Course is required every 4 years and satisfies 3 hours of law topics plus 3 hours of elective CE credit, for a total of 6 hours.

questions.question_text: A sales associate changes brokerages. What must they do?
questions.explanation: When a sales associate transfers to a new brokerage, they must notify KREC of the change. The license must be held under the new principal broker.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to stop practicing temporarily but keep their license. What option is available?
distractors: Request a practice exemption from KREC
Transfer the license to another person
questions.explanation: Kentucky allows licensees to place their license in escrow (inactive status) while not practicing. This preserves the license without requiring active practice or full CE compliance.

questions.question_text: A licensee changes their home address. What must they do?
distractors: No action required for address changes
questions.explanation: Licensees must report address, name, or brokerage changes to KREC. This keeps the Commission's records accurate for communication and enforcement purposes.

questions.question_text: An out-of-state licensee wants to practice in Kentucky. What is available to them?
distractors: They must complete full KY pre-license education
questions.explanation: Kentucky may recognize licenses from other states under certain conditions. This is not automatic reciprocity — the applicant must meet KREC's requirements for recognition.

questions.question_text: A licensee with an escrowed license decides to return to active practice. What must they do?
questions.correct_answer: Complete CE and apply for reactivation
distractors: Simply notify their new principal broker
Pay only the applicable renewal fee
questions.explanation: Reactivating an escrowed license requires completing any outstanding CE requirements and applying for reactivation through KREC, along with applicable fees.
